key_insight: The paper extends continuous-time mean field control to settings where dynamics and rewards depend on both state and control distributions, and derives a deterministic-policy actor-critic gradient that works in this extended measure-dependent setting.
engineering_relevance: It enables model-free reinforcement learning for complex coupled multi-agent systems and control problems with distributional feedback, such as consensus formation and liquidation with crowding effects.
method: Derive a model-free sensitivity formula for parameterized McKean–Vlasov dynamics.
Build a deterministic policy gradient using advantage-rate functions on Wasserstein space, with local value/advantage representations that include measure derivatives.

Implement a continuous-time deep deterministic policy gradient algorithm with particle approximations, measure-dependent neural networks, temporal-difference learning, and exploration in action or parameter space.
evidence_signal: The method is evaluated in numerical experiments on stochastic Cucker–Smale consensus control and optimal liquidation with trade crowding, showing efficiency, stability, and robustness.
watch_item: The approach relies on particle approximations and measure-derivative estimation, which can be computationally demanding and sensitive to approximation error in high-dimensional settings.
paper_title: Actor-Critic Learning for Extended Mean Field Control with Deterministic Policies
